title: Crossoglossa fratrum
taxonomy: Crossoglossa fratrum is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. This genus, Crossoglossa, is a small group of orchids primarily found in Central and South America. The classification of Crossoglossa fratrum is further specified within the subfamily Zygopetalinae. Members of the Crossoglossa genus are known for their unique, often intricate flowers and are typically epiphytic. Crossoglossa fratrum, in particular, is a relatively rare species within its genus.

intro: Crossoglossa fratrum, also known as the "Fraternity's Tongue," is a species of flowering plant in the family Araceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Crossoglossa fratrum include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, this plant is used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and skin irritations.
